title: Lens K8s Champion (Mirantis)
description: Recognized as a Lens K8s Champion for actively supporting the Kubernetes and cloud-native community and creating practical content.

title: Microsoft MVP
description: I was recognized as a Microsoft Most Valuable Professional (MVP) in the Developer Technologies category (Technology Area: DevOps) for my contributions to the developer community from writing blogs, videos, and speaking at events.

title: Docker Captain of the Year
description: I was honored with the Docker Captain of the Year award, recognizing my contributions to the Docker community. This award was presented during Docker Captains Summit 2025 in Istanbul, Turkey.

title: Featured in Docker Captain Interview
description: Featured in Docker's official blog as part of their "From the Captain's Chair" series. Shared my journey from learning about databases to becoming a Docker Captain, my contributions to the community, and insights on Docker's latest features.

title: Joined Kestra as a QAE
description: I joined Kestra as a Quality Assurance Engineer. I test new features, run automated checks, and identify bugs to ensure software quality across both open source and enterprise editions.

title: Delivered a talk at KubeCon India
description: I delivered a talk at KubeCon India, a premier conference focusing on Kubernetes and Cloud Native technologies. I spoke about WebAssembly and Docker adoption and development process.

title: Featured on maintaine.rs Book
description: I was featured on maintaine.rs book, a collection of stories from maintainers in open source world. I was featured for my work as a maintainer and contributor to the open source community.

title: Delivered my first talk at International Conference
description: It was my first time attending and delivering a talk at an international conference, KubeCon China in Hong Kong. I spoke about WebAssembly and Docker development.

image.alt: CNCF Cloud Native Computing Foundation logo

title: Featured on Official Open Source Org Website
description: As a maintainer, I was highlighted during Maintainer Month, a campaign to honor the individuals who steward and sustain Open Source projects. I was featured on the official Open Source Org website sharing my journey, contributions I made and how I got started with open source.

title: Delivered a talk at Linux Foundation event
description: I delivered a talk at the SOSS Community Day, India (KubeCon India), hosted by the Linux Foundation/CNCF. This was the first event of its kind in India. I delivered a talk on Software Supply Chain Security and how to improve it by using Docker Scout in the CI/CD pipeline. Without any doubt, this was the best talk I have ever delivered.

image.alt: CNCF Cloud Native Computing Foundation logo

link.ariaLabel: Watch SOSS Community Day talk video

title: Published first blog on FreeCodeCamp
description: Last year I got approved as a writer at FreeCodeCamp. Finally got some time to write a detailed blog apart from my regular writing on Dev.to. The blog was about how Feature Flags can be helpful in backend development like building APIs. This is the longest blog I have ever written with 4k words and a whopping ~18 min read time.

title: Part of Docker Official Docs team
description: I was invited to official Docker docs team as a Docker Captain. I am one of few people who are working on the official Docker docs. I am helping the team to improve the docs, adding language-specific guides, how-to guides and more.

title: Docker Captain
description: It was a long time coming. I was recognized as a Docker Captain for my contributions to the Docker community. This is a huge achievement as a Docker fanatic. I work closely with different teams at Docker to improve the product and help the community.

title: Contributed to Official GitHub Open Source Guide
description: I and other maintainers came together and worked with GitHub to write a guide on how to maintain balance for open source maintainers and avoid burnout.

title: Invited as a Guest Speaker at NIT Patna
description: I was invited as a guest speaker at NIT Patna to deliver a talk on open source and how to effectively maintain a open source project. This was the place where I delivered my first in-person talk last year.

title: 10K Followers on Dev.to
description: I reached 10K followers on Dev.to. I was actively writing articles and helping others to get started with open source and devops. At this time my total views were around 150K.

title: DevOps repo crossed 2.5K Stars
description: DevOps repository crossed 2.5K stars on GitHub. Every week thousands of devs are visiting site and GitHub repo to learn about DevOps and how to get started with it. The repo is used by people from companies like Google, Microsoft, Amazon and more. Even some are using it as a reference in their interviews.

title: 100K Views on dev.to My Articles
description: I never thought people would be interested in reading my articles. I reached 100K views on my articles on Dev.to. Total views including other platforms are 135K. I mostly write about open source devops and everything in between. And got this many views just after writing 10-12 articles.

title: Top Author and Mod 2023 at Dev.to
description: I was recognized as the top author and moderator at Dev.to for the year 2023. I was actively writing articles and helping platform to moderate the content.

title: 1000 GitHub Followers
description: I reached 1000 followers on GitHub. I was actively contributing to open source and helping others to get started with open source.

title: Delivered my first conference talk
description: I delivered my first conference talk at Web3Conf. I talked about how to get started with open source and how to maintain an open source software being a maintainer. The room was almost houseful and it was an unforgettable experience.

title: Dev Advocate at Livecycle
description: I joined Livecycle as a Dev Advocate. I was deeply involved in our open source offering "Preevy" and helped developers to get started with it by creating content and delivering talks.

title: 10K Followers on LinkedIn
description: I reached 10K followers on LinkedIn. Started as diversifying my social media presence and people supported here as well. The quality of engagement was much better than Twitter.

title: Delivered my first in-person talk
description: I delivered my first in-person talk at API Days Patna at NIT Patna. It was a meet-up. I talked about how using Docker and Docker Compose we can develop and test our APIs locally. Also, this was my first time hosting an event. I co-hosted the event with another Postman Student Leader.

title: Kubernetes and SIGs Org Member
description: I became a member of Kubernetes and Kubernetes SIGs GitHub Org. I was contributing to the Kubernetes Website project and helping in the area of documentation, triaging issues and PRs.

title: 10K Followers on Twitter
description: I reached 10K followers on Twitter. I was regularly posting about open source and learning I was doing. It was a great milestone for me.

title: First Freelancing Gig
description: I got my first freelancing gig from a open source cloud company. It was a Sponsored post on my Twitter account. The gig was about sharing about their open source project. This is time I realized that I can make money from my social media presence.

title: First GitHub Sponsor
description: I got my first GitHub sponsor. This feeling was a bit overwhelming because I never expected that someone would sponsor me for my work. They were super generous and sponsored me.

title: Postman Student Leader
description: For spreading the voice and educating students about APIs and Postman, I was recognized as a Postman Student Leader.

title: 100 GitHub Stars
description: open-source-with-pradumna repository reached 100 stars on GitHub. This was huge because I never expected that my repository would help people get started with open source and hit 100 stars.

title: EddieHub Ambassador
description: As I was contributing to the EddieHub community for a while and passionate about open source, I was selected as an EddieHub Ambassador. There were only 7 ambassadors at that time. I retained my position as an ambassador for next batch as well.

title: Crossed 1K Followers on Twitter (X)
description: I crossed 1k followers on Twitter. I never imagined in a million years that people would be interested in listening to my thoughts and journey. Crazy! because I never liked social media in general.

title: "Open Source with Pradumna"
description: Open Source With Pradumna started as a 15-day bootcamp of Twitter posts (Threads) where I was sharing my journey of open source and how to get started with it. This was a turning point for me. Fun fact: It all started from nowhere. I was on bed rest after I screwed up my foot in a bike accident and had nothing to do. After this my social media presence grew, and a lot of people started knowing me.

title: Won my first Hackathon (Solo)
description: I won my first hackathon with daily.dev, which was global. I build a Discord bot that uses RSS feeds to get the bookmarked articles from daily.dev and post them to the Discord server. Also I got mentioned in the Newsletter.

title: Won Postman Hackathon
description: Postman Hosted a hackathon called API Fest 2022. I with a team of two, we build a fully functional API from scratch using Node.js and MongoDB. We won the hackathon and got a chance to present our project to the Postman team.

title: Delivered my first talk
description: First time I got invite to deliver a talk. It was on open source and how to get started with it. It was virtually and the fact that I delivered the talk via my $100 phone because laptop mic and camera was broken and battery don't work. Check the link for the phone screenshot I posted on Twitter.

title: Got mentioned in a Newsletter
description: First time I got a mention in a Newsletter. It was from EddieHub. I was mentioned for congratulating me becoming a Postman Student Expert.

title: Open Source Maintainer
description: This was my first experience as a maintainer. I became a maintainer at EddieHub because I was contributing to the community projects and helping others on the GitHub Org as well as Discord.

title: Started Open Source
description: It all started with a website asking for a GitHub profile and I didn't know what it's all about. Then I dove into it and started contributing to open source. I started with fixing typos, broken links and documentation.

title: Featured on an Article with ShareIT COO
description: One of China's leading publishers of tech news, Tiger sniffing group wrote an article on ShareIT and I was featured in that with the COO (Json Wang) of ShareIt. The article was about how ShareIT is helping people to share files without internet. The main article was published in China in Mandarin language.
